narrative.context: Your internship tasks feel repetitive. You feel bored and slightly frustrated, and your focus drops.
narrative.prompt: How can you use these emotions to think and act more effectively?

options.label: Treat frustration as a signal to adjust strategy
options.description: Identify what’s missing (challenge/clarity). Propose one improved task or learning goal to your mentor.

ei.rationale: Uses emotion as information for problem definition and goal planning—emotion facilitates thinking.

options.label: Power through without reflection
options.description: Finish tasks mechanically and hope motivation returns.

ei.rationale: Maintains output, but doesn’t use emotion to guide improved thinking or engagement.

options.label: Complain to peers repeatedly
options.description: Vent often, but don’t change your plan.

ei.rationale: Emotion becomes repetitive rumination; little cognitive benefit or planning.

options.label: Quit impulsively
options.description: Resign suddenly because it feels pointless.

ei.rationale: Emotion drives abrupt action without analysis; thinking is not facilitated.
